source.description: Built-in image generation created a six-panel pencil process contact sheet for the stack-of-books lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet was reviewed before cropping; the final panel is used as the finished asset.

process_strategy: The six frames keep one slightly offset book stack, introducing each book block, page edges, bookmark, cover bands, and soft shadow before the final graphite pass.

final_elements.name: bottom book block
final_elements.description: A wide bottom book creates the base of the stack.

final_elements.name: middle book block
final_elements.description: A second book sits slightly offset on top of the bottom book.

final_elements.name: top book block
final_elements.description: A smaller top book completes the stack.

final_elements.name: page edges
final_elements.description: Thin page lines run along the visible book sides.

final_elements.name: ribbon bookmark
final_elements.description: A narrow ribbon bookmark hangs from the top book.

final_elements.name: cover bands and corner wear
final_elements.description: Simple cover bands, corner marks, and worn edges make the books feel used.

final_elements.name: soft cast shadow
final_elements.description: A light graphite shadow sits under the book stack.

frames.visible_job: Block in the bottom and middle book shapes with light perspective guides.
Add the smaller top book and refine the offset stack angles.
Add thin page-edge lines along the visible sides of each book.
Draw the narrow ribbon bookmark hanging from the top book.
Add cover bands, corner wear, and a soft shadow under the stack.

final_step.visible_job: Darken keeper lines, clarify the page edges and bookmark, and add restrained graphite shading to the existing books and shadow.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
